body:
## Problem

`.github/workflows/preview.yml` triggers on both `pull_request.opened` and `pull_request.labeled`, and both share one concurrency group with `cancel-in-progress: true`:

```yaml
concurrency:
  group: sepo-preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

When a PR is opened, the auto-added `agent` status label fires a `labeled` event almost immediately. That `labeled` run enters the **same** concurrency group and **cancels the in-progress `opened` deploy** — then the `labeled` run itself **skips**, because the deploy gate only allows label-triggered deploys for the `sepo-preview` label:

```yaml
github.event.action != 'labeled' || github.event.label.name == 'sepo-preview'
```

Sequence: valid `opened` deploy → **cancelled**; non-preview `agent`-label run → **skipped**; result → **no preview deployed**. (The deploy condition itself was fine — same-repo, `agent/` branch, public repo — so this is purely the concurrency interaction.)

## Fix

Make `cancel-in-progress` conditional so label events never cancel a live deploy:

```yaml
concurrency:
  group: sepo-preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event.action != 'labeled' }}
```

A real push (`synchronize`) still supersedes a stale in-flight deploy; a label event now queues behind the live `opened` run (and harmlessly skips) instead of killing it. (Alternative: give ignored-label runs their own concurrency group.)

## Acceptance

- Opening an `agent/` PR deploys a preview; the auto `agent` label no longer cancels it.
- A later push still supersedes a stale deploy.
- The `sepo-preview` opt-in label still triggers a deploy.

## Note

If the `workflow_run` split (#12) lands first, the same trigger/concurrency shape moves to the build workflow — carry this conditional `cancel-in-progress` there too.
title: Preview workflow: the auto `agent` label cancels the live deploy (concurrency bug)
